channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Tanzanian MP Selemani Bungara Dies After Long Illness
item.description: Former Tanzanian Member of Parliament Selemani Bungara, famously known as Bwege, passed away on March 30, 2026, in Dar es Salaam while undergoing treatment at EM Hospital. He had been struggling with ...

item.title: Nigerian Army Secures Key Towns, Repels Insurgent Attacks in March 2026
item.description: For March 2026, the Nigerian Army show strong courage and resilience as dem dey fight insurgency for North-East Nigeria. Troops celebrate Eid-el-Fitr with Ngoshe residents after securing Forward Opera...

item.title: Megan Fox Blocks Machine Gun Kelly on Instagram Amid Reports of Breakup Finalization
item.description: Megan Fox, aged 39, has allegedly blocked her former fiancé Machine Gun Kelly, 35, on Instagram, signaling that their on-again, off-again relationship might finally be over. The action reportedly occu...

item.title: Teenagers’ sentences reduced in Surrey mechanic murder case following appeal
item.description: The life sentences of two teenagers convicted in the stabbing death of Surrey mechanic Paul Prestbakmo have been overturned on appeal. Prestbakmo, a well-known mechanic, was killed on August 16, 2019,...

item.title: Kevin Rudd bids farewell to US embassy staff but plans to remain active in US-China relations
item.description: Kevin Rudd has concluded his three-year term as Australia’s Ambassador to the United States, departing the post this week. In a farewell email sent to embassy staff on 12 January, the former prime min...

item.title: Public anger and digital mourning: understanding Tanzania's collective woundedness
item.description: The article by Shimbo Pastory examines the growing trend of online mockery and anger directed at both living and deceased political leaders in Tanzania, suggesting it points to a deeper societal wound...

item.title: Eskom posts profits amid rising electricity tariffs
item.description: Despite generating over 5% less electricity in March 2026 compared to the same month in 2022, Eskom has reported a second consecutive year of profitability. From March to December 2025, revenue reache...

item.title: Caster Semenya challenges IOC's genetic testing policy for female athletes
item.description: South African middle-distance runner Caster Semenya is taking a strong stand against the International Olympic Committee's (IOC) latest policy that requires genetic screening for female athletes. The ...

item.title: WA mines near fuel shortages as RBA removes credit card surcharges
item.description: In today’s Newsworthy episode, Ben O’Shea reports that several Western Australian mines are facing a serious fuel shortage, with supplies expected to run out in just ten days. The situation could have...

item.title: Planned track closures on south-east Queensland trains for maintenance and upgrades
item.description: South-east Queensland's rail network is set to experience a series of track closures over the next month due to essential maintenance and upgrades. The timing comes as residents are being encouraged t...

item.title: ED challenges Kejriwal’s acquittal over non-appearance in liquor policy case
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Monday approached the Delhi High Court to challenge the acquittal of Aam Aadmi Party chief Arvind Kejriwal in two cases related to his non-appearance before the age...

item.title: Six Held as DCI Investigates Unauthorized Mass Grave in Kericho
item.description: Investigations into a mass grave in Kericho County have intensified, with six people now held in custody as the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI), led by Mohamed Amin, seeks to uncover how ...

item.title: Pakistan Emphasises Mediating Role in Iran-US Dialogue Amid Rising Regional Tensions
item.description: Pakistan’s Ambassador to the United States, Rizwan Saeed Sheikh, has reiterated Islamabad’s dedication to facilitating dialogue between the US and Iran, even as tensions in the region remain high. In ...

item.title: Butter-coated soft serve stirs debate over taste and health at Sydney Easter Show
item.description: At the Sydney Royal Easter Show, a new butter-dipped soft serve ice-cream has captured public attention — not so much for its flavour, but for its Instagram-worthy appearance. The treat features a swi...

item.title: Horoscope Predictions for Each Zodiac Sign on March 31, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s daily horoscope for March 31, 2026, written by Sally Brompton, delivers insights for all twelve zodiac signs. The piece opens with advice for people whose birthday falls on this d...

item.title: Ford South Africa issues recall for Transit and Tourneo vehicles due to potential battery gas hazard
item.description: Ford South Africa has announced a recall of 582 Transit and Tourneo Custom vehicles, covering both current and previous generations sold between 2022 and 2026. The decision follows a notification from...

item.title: DPM Ishaq Dar visits China to discuss regional peace and bilateral ties
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has departed for China on a one-day official visit at the invitation of Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. This visit comes as Pakistan intensifies ...

item.title: Rajasthan High Court criticises changes to transgender rights under the 2026 Amendment Bill
item.description: The Rajasthan High Court has raised serious constitutional concerns over the 2026 Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, observing that it turns a basic human right — the right to ...

item.title: Nollywood and Nigeria Cinema: How Local Films Fit in the Market
item.description: For Nigeria, e be like say we dey watch as foreign films chop our cinema market while local movies dey struggle. In 2025, Warner Bros. and Disney dey control over 66 percent of the Nigerian and Anglop...

item.title: Pakistan and Other Nations Protest Israeli Restrictions on Religious Worship in Jerusalem
item.description: Pakistan, together with seven other countries including Egypt, Jordan, UAE, Indonesia, Turkiye, Saudi Arabia and Qatar, has strongly condemned Israel's restrictions on the freedom of worship for Musli...
